extraction.one_line_summary: MATTHEWS Michael won the sprint after Jayco AlUla's sustained climbing pace dropped McNulty and traditional sprint leadout trains, with Pedersen recovering from being dropped on climbs
tactical_patterns: Jayco AlUla set climbing tempo specifically to thin sprint field rather than protect breakaway, revealing GC-agnostic sprint preparation strategy.
Evenepoel actively contested intermediate bonus seconds despite holding pink jersey, signaling aggressive accumulation strategy before shedding leader duties.
Pinot collecting KOM points via repeated attacks rather than breakaway commitment suggests targeting classification without sacrificing GC support role.

rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: won after Jayco's climbing work filtered the sprint field, showing renewed ability to survive lumpy parcours that eliminated traditional fastmen
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: dropped on climbs but regained peloton to contest sprint, demonstrating unexpected recovery capacity on difficult terrain
rider_signals.McNULTY Brandon: negative: unexpectedly distanced on modest third-category climbs while teammates controlled pace, major red flag for GC ambitions
rider_signals.KÄMNA Lennard: negative: absent from finale on terrain suited to his profile while teammate Matthews contended, suggests poor form or tactical misallocation
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: neutral: attacked twice for KOM points then returned to bunch, indicating classification hunting without committing to breakaway risk
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: neutral: caught in crash but recovered to peloton, showing strong chase capacity but raising durability concerns
